---
title: "HM_hpulchrum_new"
format: html
editor: visual
---

# RADseq Analysis Workflow

## **1 Demultiplexing and Cleaning**

Script: /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Scripts/demultiplexing_new_hpu.sh

``` bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash

#SBATCH --job-name=demux_HM
#SBATCH --time=30:00:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=8
#SBATCH --mem=400G
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --mail-type=END,FAIL
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/logs/%x_%j.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/logs/%x_%j.err

# ============================================================
# PURPOSE
# ============================================================
# This pipeline:
#
# 1. Trims the 5 bp spacer from R1 only
# 2. Demultiplexes reads using process_radtags
# 3. Optionally removes PCR duplicates AFTER demultiplexing
#
# Library structure identified:
#
#   NNNNN + BARCODE + GGTACC + genomic DNA
#
# where:
#   NNNNN = diversity spacer (5 bp)
#   BARCODE = inline barcode (6 bp)
#   GGTACC = KpnI restriction site
#
# ============================================================

set -euo pipefail
shopt -s nullglob

# ============================================================
# LOAD MODULES
# ============================================================

module purge

module load bio/Stacks/2.62-foss-2022a
module load bio/cutadapt/4.2-GCCcore-11.3.0

# ============================================================
# CONFIGURATION
# ============================================================

THREADS=8

FASTQ_DIR="/scratch/RADseq_ag-deamux/NGS_data_DX620_26047"
OUTROOT="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/new"
mkdir -p "$OUTROOT"

BARCODE_FILE="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/barcodes_list_HM.txt"

R1="$FASTQ_DIR/DX620_338918_26O47-1_S1_L000_R1_001.fastq.gz"
R2="$FASTQ_DIR/DX620_338918_26O47-1_S1_L000_R2_001.fastq.gz"

ENZYME="kpnI"

# process_radtags options
INLINE_FLAG="--inline_null"
BARCODE_MISMATCH="--barcode-dist-1 1"

# ============================================================
# OUTPUT DIRECTORIES
# ============================================================

LOG_DIR="$OUTROOT/logs"
TRIM_DIR="$OUTROOT/trimmed"
RAD_DIR="$OUTROOT/radtags"
DUP_DIR="$OUTROOT/clone_filtered"

mkdir -p \
    "$LOG_DIR" \
    "$TRIM_DIR" \
    "$RAD_DIR" \
    "$DUP_DIR"

# ============================================================
# CHECK INPUT FILES
# ============================================================

if [[ ! -f "$R1" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: R1 file not found"
    exit 1
fi

if [[ ! -f "$R2" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: R2 file not found"
    exit 1
fi

if [[ ! -f "$BARCODE_FILE" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: Barcode file not found"
    exit 1
fi

echo "=================================================="
echo "JOB STARTED: $(date)"
echo "HOSTNAME: $(hostname)"
echo "=================================================="

# ============================================================
# STEP 1 — TRIM 5 bp SPACER FROM R1 ONLY
# ============================================================
#
# Your data show:
#
#   position 6 = barcode
#   position 12 = GGTACC
#
# meaning:
#
#   5 bp spacer + 6 bp barcode + restriction site
#
# We therefore remove first 5 bp from R1 only.
#
# R2 remains untouched.
#
# ============================================================

echo "=================================================="
echo "STEP 1: TRIMMING R1 SPACER"
echo "=================================================="

TRIM_R1="$TRIM_DIR/trimmed_R1.fastq.gz"
TRIM_R2="$TRIM_DIR/trimmed_R2.fastq.gz"

cutadapt \
    -u 5 \
    -o "$TRIM_R1" \
    -p "$TRIM_R2" \
    "$R1" \
    "$R2" \
    > "$LOG_DIR/cutadapt.log" 2>&1

echo "Spacer trimming completed"

# ============================================================
# STEP 2 — DEMULTIPLEX WITH process_radtags
# ============================================================

echo "=================================================="
echo "STEP 2: process_radtags"
echo "=================================================="

process_radtags \
    -1 "$TRIM_R1" \
    -2 "$TRIM_R2" \
    -o "$RAD_DIR" \
    -b "$BARCODE_FILE" \
    -e "$ENZYME" \
    $INLINE_FLAG \
    $BARCODE_MISMATCH \
    -r -c -q \
    -i gzfastq \
    -T "$THREADS" \
    -D \
    > "$LOG_DIR/process_radtags.log" 2>&1

echo "process_radtags completed"

# ============================================================
# STEP 3 — OPTIONAL PCR DUPLICATE REMOVAL
# ============================================================
#
# This is performed AFTER demultiplexing,
# which is the biologically cleaner workflow.
#
# ============================================================

echo "=================================================="
echo "STEP 3: clone_filter"
echo "=================================================="

DEMUX_R1=("$RAD_DIR"/*.1.fq.gz)

if [[ ${#DEMUX_R1[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: No demultiplexed files found"
    exit 1
fi

for r1_file in "${DEMUX_R1[@]}"; do

    sample=$(basename "$r1_file" .1.fq.gz)

    r2_file="$RAD_DIR/${sample}.2.fq.gz"

    if [[ ! -f "$r2_file" ]]; then
        echo "WARNING: Missing R2 for sample $sample"
        continue
    fi

    sample_out="$DUP_DIR/$sample"

    mkdir -p "$sample_out"

    echo "Processing clone_filter for sample: $sample"

    clone_filter \
        -1 "$r1_file" \
        -2 "$r2_file" \
        -o "$sample_out" \
        -i gzfastq \
        --oligo-len-1 6 \
        -y gzfastq \
        > "$sample_out/clone_filter.log" 2>&1
done

echo "=================================================="
echo "PIPELINE FINISHED SUCCESSFULLY"
echo "DATE: $(date)"
echo "=================================================="
```

## 2 Quality Control and Read Distribution Analysis

#### Summarising TSV

``` bash
{bash}
# Extract the per_barcode_raw_read_counts section and create a TSV
sed -n '/BEGIN per_barcode_raw_read_counts/,/END per_barcode_raw_read_counts/p' /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/new/radtags/process_radtags.trimmed.log | \
grep -v "BEGIN\|END" > /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/new/radtags/per_sample_counts.tsv
```

After that, make the figures for the Proportion of Library, Retained Reads (M) and Proportion of Retained Reads

#### Plotting

``` r
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

# Plot Stacks-Process Radtags per-sample statistics

## Input data

### Path to output PDFs
pdfPath = '/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/new/pdf'

dir.create(pdfPath, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

### Path to `per_sample_counts.tsv` file
procRadtagsLogPath = '/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/new/radtags/per_sample_counts.tsv'

### Open the file as a dataframe
processRadtags = read.delim(procRadtagsLogPath)

### Check column names
print(colnames(processRadtags))  # Optional debug

### Add the population identifier (extract it from the sample names)
processRadtags$PopID <- sapply(strsplit(processRadtags$Filename, '_'),
                               function(x){x[2]})

## Calculate overall statistics
totalReads <- sum(processRadtags$Total)
totalRetained <- sum(processRadtags$Retained.Reads)
percRetained <- totalRetained / totalReads

## Plots

### Initialize PDF
pdf(paste0(pdfPath, '/process_radtags_stats.pdf'), width=8, height=8)

### A. Proportion of Library

#### Calculate percentage of total reads
processRadtags$PercTotal <- processRadtags$Total / totalReads
summary(processRadtags$PercTotal)
sd(processRadtags$PercTotal)

#### Sort the dataframe by the percent total
processRadtags <- processRadtags[order(processRadtags$PercTotal),]

#### Plot dotchart
dotchart(processRadtags$PercTotal,
         labels = processRadtags$Filename,
         xlab='',
         xlim=c(0,0.08),
         cex=0.7)
title(xlab='Proportion of Library', line=2)
abline(v=mean(processRadtags$PercTotal), lty=2, col='#b22222')

#### Per-population Boxplot
boxplot(PercTotal~PopID, data=processRadtags,
        las=1,
        xlab='',
        ylab='',
        col=c("#E69F00", "#56B4E9"),
        horizontal = TRUE,
        ylim=c(0,0.08))
title(xlab='Proportion of Library', line=2)
title(ylab='Population', line=2)

### B. Reads per-sample

#### Stats
summary(processRadtags$Retained.Reads)
sd(processRadtags$Retained.Reads)

#### Sort
processRadtags <- processRadtags[order(processRadtags$Retained.Reads),]

#### Dotchart
dotchart(processRadtags$Retained.Reads / 1e6,
         labels = processRadtags$Filename,
         xlab='',
         xlim=c(0, max(processRadtags$Retained.Reads) / 1e6),
         cex=0.7)
title(xlab='Retained Reads (M)', line=2)
abline(v=mean(processRadtags$Retained.Reads / 1e6), lty=2, col='#b22222')

#### Per-population boxplot
par(mar=c(3,3,0.5,0.5) + 0.1)
boxplot(Retained.Reads / 1e6 ~ PopID, data=processRadtags,
        las=1,
        xlab='',
        ylab='',
        col=c("#E69F00", "#56B4E9"),
        horizontal = TRUE,
        ylim=c(0, max(processRadtags$Retained.Reads / 1e6)))
title(xlab='Retained Reads (M)', line=2)
title(ylab='Population', line=2)

### C. Percent retained

#### Calculate percent retained
processRadtags$PercRetained <- processRadtags$Retained.Reads / processRadtags$Total
summary(processRadtags$PercRetained)
sd(processRadtags$PercRetained)

#### Sort
processRadtags <- processRadtags[order(processRadtags$PercRetained),]

#### Dotchart
dotchart(processRadtags$PercRetained,
         labels = processRadtags$Filename,
         xlab='',
         xlim=c(0.4,1),
         cex=0.7)
title(xlab='Proportion of Retained Reads', line=2)
abline(v=mean(processRadtags$PercRetained), lty=2, col='#b22222')

#### Per-population Boxplot
boxplot(PercRetained~PopID, data=processRadtags,
        las=1,
        xlab='',
        ylab='',
        col=c("#E69F00", "#56B4E9"),
        horizontal = TRUE,
        ylim=c(0.4,1))
title(xlab='Proportion of Retained Reads', line=2)
title(ylab='Population', line=2)

### Close PDF
dev.off()
```

## 3 FastP

This was done with the process_radtags files

Script: fastp_new_hmhpu.sh

``` bash
#!/bin/bash -l

#SBATCH --job-name=fastp_HM_new
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=4
#SBATCH --mem=16G
#SBATCH --time=02:00:00
#SBATCH --array=1-51
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/new/logs/%x_%A_%a.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/new/logs/%x_%A_%a.err

# --------------------
# Directories
# --------------------
INDIR="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/new/radtags"
OUTDIR="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/new/fastp"
mkdir -p "$OUTDIR"

# --------------------
# Build sample list from forward reads
# --------------------
SAMPLE_LIST=($(ls ${INDIR}/*.1.fq.gz | grep -v '\.rem' | xargs -n1 basename | sed 's/\.1\.fq\.gz//'))
SAMPLE=${SAMPLE_LIST[$SLURM_ARRAY_TASK_ID-1]}

# --------------------
# Input files
# --------------------
FQ1=${INDIR}/${SAMPLE}.1.fq.gz
FQ2=${INDIR}/${SAMPLE}.2.fq.gz

# --------------------
# Output files
# --------------------
OUT1=${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}_R1.fastp.fq.gz
OUT2=${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}_R2.fastp.fq.gz
HTML=${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}.fastp.html
JSON=${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}.fastp.json

# --------------------
# Run FastP
# --------------------
fastp \
  -i "$FQ1" \
  -I "$FQ2" \
  -o "$OUT1" \
  -O "$OUT2" \
  --detect_adapter_for_pe \
  --thread $SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK \
  --qualified_quality_phred 20 \
  --length_required 50 \
  --dedup \
  --html "$HTML" \
  --json "$JSON"
```

``` bash
module load bio/MultiQC
multiqc . -o fastp_summary
```

## 4 FastQC

Doing FastQC on the clone_filter

Script: fastqc_clone_new.sh

``` bash
#!/bin/bash -l

#SBATCH --job-name=fastqc_clone_new
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --time=2:00:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=4
#SBATCH --mem=16gb
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/new/logs/%x_%j.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/new/logs/%x_%j.err

module purge
module load bio/FastQC

BASE_DIR="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/new/clone_filtered"
OUT_DIR="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/new/clone_filtered/fastqc"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

for SAMPLE_DIR in "$BASE_DIR"/*; do
    # Only directories, skip *.rem
    if [[ -d "$SAMPLE_DIR" ]] && [[ "$SAMPLE_DIR" != *.rem ]]; then
        echo "Processing directory: $SAMPLE_DIR"
        
        # Run FastQC on all .fq.gz files inside
        for FQ in "$SAMPLE_DIR"/*.fq.gz; do
            # Check if files exist
            if [[ -f "$FQ" ]]; then
                echo "  Running FastQC on $FQ"
                fastqc -t 4 -o "$OUT_DIR" "$FQ"
            fi
        done
    fi
done
```

``` bash
module avail multiqc
module load bio/MultiQC
multiqc . -o ./multiqc_clone_old
```

## 5 Mapping

Mapping of the reads from FastP to the reference genome of *Hypericum pulchrum*

Script: mapping_new_hm_hpu.sh

``` bash
#!/bin/bash -l

#SBATCH --job-name=map_new_hm_hpu
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --time=1:00:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=6
#SBATCH --mem=32gb
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --array=1-102
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/logs/%x_%A_%a.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/logs/%x_%A_%a.err

# Directories
INDIR="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/new/fastp/"
OUTDIR="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/new/mapping"
REF="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/ref_genomes/HM/hypericumpulchrum/hypericumpulchrum_SAMEA10241452.fasta"

# Create output directory if needed
mkdir -p $OUTDIR

# Make the sample list, if not already done
# ls *_HM_*_R*.fastp.fq.gz | sed -E 's/_R[12].fastp\.fq.gz//' > sample_list_HM.txt
# This list’s only purpose is to have an easy way to get the correct files for the species you’re using.

# Use the sample list
SAMPLE=$(sed -n "${SLURM_ARRAY_TASK_ID}p" ${INDIR}/sample_list_HM.txt)

FQ1=${INDIR}/${SAMPLE}_R1.fastp.fq.gz
FQ2=${INDIR}/${SAMPLE}_R2.fastp.fq.gz

BAM=${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}.sorted.bam

echo "Mapping sample: $SAMPLE"
echo "FQ1: $FQ1"
echo "FQ2: $FQ2"
echo "Output BAM: $BAM"

module purge
module load bio/bwa-mem2/2.2.1-intel-compilers-2023.1.0
module load bio/SAMtools/1.18-GCC-12.3.0

bwa-mem2 mem -t $SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK $REF $FQ1 $FQ2 | \
    samtools view -bS - | \
    samtools sort -@ $SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK -o $BAM -

samtools index $BAM
```

## 6 Deduplication of Mapped Reads

``` bash
#!/bin/bash -l

#SBATCH --job-name=dedup_hm_new
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --time=2:00:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=12
#SBATCH --mem=24gb
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/logs/%x_%A_%a.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/logs/%x_%A_%a.err
#SBATCH --array=1-51

set -euo pipefail

module purge
module load bio/SAMtools/1.18-GCC-12.3.0

INLIST="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/new/mapping/sample_list_HM.txt"
INDIR="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/new/mapping"
OUTDIR="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/new/mapping/dedup"

mkdir -p $OUTDIR

# Get sample name
SAMPLE=$(sed -n "${SLURM_ARRAY_TASK_ID}p" ${INLIST})

echo "Processing $SAMPLE"

# Input BAM
INBAM=${INDIR}/${SAMPLE}.sorted.bam

[[ -f $INBAM ]] || {
    echo "ERROR: missing input $INBAM"
    exit 1
}

samtools quickcheck $INBAM || {
    echo "ERROR: corrupted BAM $INBAM"
    exit 1
}

# Intermediate + final BAMs
DEDUP=${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}_sorted_dedup.bam
STATS=${OUTDIR}/${SAMPLE}_duplicates_report.txt

echo "Starting piped Samtools pipeline..."

samtools sort -n -@ 4 -O bam "$INBAM" | \
samtools fixmate -m -O bam - - | \
samtools sort -@ 4 -O bam - | \
samtools markdup -r -s -@ 4 - "$DEDUP"

echo "Pipeline finished writing. Waiting for file system sync..."
# Gives the parallel file system 5 seconds to fully write and close the BAM block
sleep 5

echo "Generating stats..."
samtools flagstat "$DEDUP" > "$STATS"

echo "Indexing..."
samtools index "$DEDUP"

echo "Finished $SAMPLE"
```

## 7 (Multi)BamQC

Run the following for all the sorted.bam files after deduplication

``` bash
for bam in *_sorted_dedup.bam; 
do
  sample=${bam%_sorted_dedup.bam}

echo "Processing $sample"

qualimap bamqc \
  --java-mem-size=8G \
  -bam "$bam" \
  -outdir "bamqc/$sample" \
  -outformat HTML
done
```

Run MultiBamQC for all samples

``` bash
#!/bin/bash -l

#SBATCH --job-name=multibamqc_hm_new
#SBATCH --time=0:15:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=2
#SBATCH --mem=8gb
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/logs/%x_%A.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/logs/%x_%A.err

set -euo pipefail

# CORRECTED PATH: Points exactly to where you ran 'ls'
BAMQC_DIR="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/new/mapping/dedup/bamqc"
MULTIBAMQC_DIR="$BAMQC_DIR/multibamqc"
INPUT_GROUPS_FILE="$BAMQC_DIR/input_groups.txt"

# Create the final summary directory if it doesn't exist
mkdir -p "$MULTIBAMQC_DIR"
rm -f "$INPUT_GROUPS_FILE"

echo "Locating existing BAMQC reports and creating input groups file..."

# Loop through your existing folders (like 216_HM_10, 271_HM_3, etc.)
for PATH_DIR in "$BAMQC_DIR"/*; do
    # Verify it's a directory, ignore the target output folder, and confirm genome_results.txt exists
    if [ -d "$PATH_DIR" ] && [ "$(basename "$PATH_DIR")" != "multibamqc" ] && [ -f "$PATH_DIR/genome_results.txt" ]; then
        NAME=$(basename "$PATH_DIR")
        echo -e "${NAME}\t${PATH_DIR}\t1" >> "$INPUT_GROUPS_FILE"
    fi
done

echo "Running Qualimap Multi-BAMQC on existing data..."
export JAVA_OPTS="-Djava.awt.headless=true"

qualimap multi-bamqc \
    -d "$INPUT_GROUPS_FILE" \
    -outdir "$MULTIBAMQC_DIR" \
    -outformat PDF:HTML \
    -nw 400

echo "Qualimap Multi-BAMQC summary report generation complete!"
```

## 8 VCF Calling

Doing VCF calling for old and new data joined

``` bash
#!/bin/bash

#SBATCH --job-name=vcf_calling
#SBATCH --time=10:00:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=8
#SBATCH --mem=64gb
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --mail-type=END,FAIL
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/logs/%x_%j.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/logs/%x_%j.err

set -euo pipefail

module purge
module load bio/BCFtools/1.19-GCC-13.2.0

# Set paths
# Referencegenome
REF="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/ref_genomes/HM/hypericumpulchrum/hypericumpulchrum_SAMEA10241452.fasta"

BAMLIST_OLD="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/old/mapping/dedup/bam_file_paths_sorted_old.txt"
BAMLIST_NEW="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/duprem/HM/new/mapping/dedup/bam_file_paths_sorted_new.txt"
BAMLIST="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/bamlist.txt"

cat "$BAMLIST_OLD" "$BAMLIST_NEW" > "$BAMLIST"

echo "Combined BAM count: $(wc -l < "$BAMLIST")" >> $SLURM_JOB_ID.vcf.log

OUTDIR="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM"

OUT="${OUTDIR}/AlandIslandsHM.vcf.gz"

mkdir -p "$OUTDIR"

# Print job info to log file
echo "Starting variant calling with bcftools" > $SLURM_JOB_ID.vcf.log
echo "Reference Genome: $REF" >> $SLURM_JOB_ID.vcf.log
echo "BAM list: $BAMLIST" >> $SLURM_JOB_ID.vcf.log
echo "Output VCF: $OUT" >> $SLURM_JOB_ID.vcf.log
echo "Start time: $(date)" >> $SLURM_JOB_ID.vcf.log

bcftools mpileup \
-f "$REF" \
-b "$BAMLIST" \
-q 20 \
-Q 30 \
-a AD,DP,SP \
-Ou \
| bcftools call \
-m -v \
-a GQ,GP \
-Oz \
-o "$OUT"

bcftools index "$OUT"

echo "End time: $(date)" >> $SLURM_JOB_ID.vcf.log
```

## 9 Pre-Visualisation

After the VCF calling I did the pre visualisation for the old and new data to make the patchwork plots.

``` bash
#!/bin/bash -l

#SBATCH --job-name=vcftools_stats
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --ntasks=1
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=2
#SBATCH --mem=8gb
#SBATCH --time=01:00:00
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --mail-type=END,FAIL
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/logs/%x_%j.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/logs/%x_%j.err

set -euo pipefail

# Load module
module purge
module load bio/VCFtools/0.1.16-GCC-11.3.0

# Make variables
SET_VCF="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/AlandIslandsHM.vcf.gz"
OUT="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools"

mkdir -p "$OUT"

echo "Starting VCFtools statistics"
echo "VCF: $SET_VCF"
echo "Output directory: $OUT"

# Calculate the allele frequency
vcftools --gzvcf $SET_VCF --freq2 --out $OUT --max-alleles 2

# Calculate the mean depth per individual
vcftools --gzvcf $SET_VCF --depth --out $OUT

# Calculate the mean depth per site
vcftools --gzvcf $SET_VCF --site-mean-depth --out $OUT

# Calculate the site quality
vcftools --gzvcf $SET_VCF --site-quality --out $OUT

# Calculate the proportion of missing data per individual
vcftools --gzvcf $SET_VCF --missing-indv --out $OUT

# Calculate proportion of missing data per site
vcftools --gzvcf $SET_VCF --missing-site --out $OUT

# Calculate the heterozygosity and inbreeding coefficient per individual
vcftools --gzvcf $SET_VCF --het --out $OUT
```

## 10 Visualisation and Patchwork Plots

``` r
install.packages("tidyverse")
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)

# Define file paths
base <- "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools"

# Helper to read tab files with header + 1 header line to skip
read_tab <- function(path, col_names) {
vroom::vroom(path, delim = "\t", skip = 1, col_names = col_names, show_col_types = FALSE)
}

# --- Read files ---
var_qual <- read_tab(file.path(base, "vcftools.lqual"),
c("CHROM","POS","QUAL"))

var_depth <- read_tab(file.path(base, "vcftools.ldepth.mean"),
c("CHROM","POS","MEAN_DEPTH","VAR_DEPTH"))

var_miss <- read_tab(file.path(base, "vcftools.lmiss"),
c("CHR","POS","N_DATA","N_GENOTYPE_FILTERED","N_MISS","F_MISS"))

# --freq2 prints allele:freq; parse the numeric frequencies correctly
var_freq_raw <- read_tab(file.path(base, "vcftools.frq"),
c("CHR","POS","N_ALLELES","N_CHR","a1","a2"))
parse_freq <- function(x) readr::parse_number(sub(".*:", "", x))
var_freq <- var_freq_raw %>%
mutate(freq1 = parse_freq(a1),
freq2 = parse_freq(a2),
maf = pmin(freq1, freq2, na.rm = TRUE))

ind_depth <- read_tab(file.path(base, "vcftools.idepth"),
c("INDV","N_SITES","MEAN_DEPTH"))

ind_miss <- read_tab(file.path(base, "vcftools.imiss"),
c("INDV","N_DATA","N_GENOTYPES_FILTERED","N_MISS","F_MISS"))

ind_het <- read_tab(file.path(base, "vcftools.het"),
c("INDV","O(HOM)","E(HOM)","N_SITES","F")) # F ~ inbreeding coefficient

write.csv(var_qual, file.path(base, "var_qual_output.csv"))
write.csv(var_depth, file.path(base, "var_depth_output.csv"))
write.csv(var_miss, file.path(base, "var_miss_output.csv"))
write.csv(var_freq, file.path(base, "var_freq_output.csv"))
write.csv(ind_depth, file.path(base, "ind_depth_output.csv"))
write.csv(ind_miss, file.path(base, "ind_miss_output.csv"))
write.csv(ind_het, file.path(base, "ind_het_output.csv”))

# Patchwork Plot

library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

# Define file paths
base <-  "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools"

# Helper to read CSV files
read_csv <- function(path) {
read.csv(path)
}

# --- Read files (now using the CSV output files) ---
var_qual <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_qual_output.csv"))
var_depth <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_depth_output.csv"))
var_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_miss_output.csv"))
var_freq <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_freq_output.csv"))
ind_depth <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_depth_output.csv"))
ind_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_miss_output.csv"))
ind_het <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_het_output.csv"))

# Quick sanity: confirm the data frames exist & have rows
for (nm in c("var_qual","var_depth","var_miss","var_freq","ind_depth","ind_miss","ind_het")) {
if (!exists(nm)) stop(sprintf("Object '%s' does not exist (check file paths in qc-setup).", nm))
if (!is.data.frame(get(nm)) || nrow(get(nm)) == 0) stop(sprintf("Object '%s' has 0 rows.", nm))
}

# Create plots for each dataset
p1 <- ggplot(var_qual, aes(QUAL)) +
geom_density(alpha = .3) +
coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 800)) +
theme_light() +
labs(title = "Variant Quality")

p2 <- ggplot(var_depth, aes(MEAN_DEPTH)) +
geom_density(alpha = .3) +
coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 75)) +
theme_light() +
labs(title = "Variant Depth (mean)")

p3 <- ggplot(var_miss, aes(F_MISS)) +
geom_density(alpha = .3) +
theme_light() +
labs(title = "Variant Missingness (fmiss)")

p4 <- ggplot(var_freq, aes(maf)) +
geom_density(alpha = .3) +
theme_light() +
labs(title = "Minor Allele Frequency (MAF)")

p5 <- ggplot(ind_depth, aes(MEAN_DEPTH)) +
geom_histogram(bins = 50, alpha = .3) +
theme_light() +
labs(title = "Individual Depth")

p6 <- ggplot(ind_miss, aes(F_MISS)) +
geom_histogram(bins = 50, alpha = .3) +
theme_light() +
labs(title = "Individual Missingness")

p7 <- ggplot(ind_het, aes(F)) +
geom_histogram(bins = 50, alpha = .3) +
theme_light() +
labs(title = "Individual Inbreeding (F)")

# Compose robustly (avoids relying on | and / operators)
panel <- patchwork::wrap_plots(list(p1, p2, p3, p4, p5, p6, p7), ncol = 2, guides = "collect")


#save panel as pdf file
ggsave( "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/vcf_raw_stats.pdf", panel, width = 10, height = 14)
```

## 11 Summary TSV of Raw VCF Stats

``` r
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(purrr)
library(readr)
library(knitr)

# Helper function to compute full summary stats for a numeric vector
summ <- function(x) {
  tibble(
    n      = sum(!is.na(x)),
    min    = min(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    q1     = quantile(x, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    median = median(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean   = mean(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    q3     = quantile(x, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    max    = max(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd     = sd(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
}

# Define file paths
base <- "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools"

# Helper to read CSV files
read_csv <- function(path) {
  read.csv(path)
}

# --- Read files (now using the CSV output files) ---
var_qual <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_qual_output.csv"))
var_depth <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_depth_output.csv"))
var_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_miss_output.csv"))
var_freq <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_freq_output.csv"))
ind_depth <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_depth_output.csv"))
ind_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_miss_output.csv"))
ind_het <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_het_output.csv"))

# Build summary for each dataset
qc_full_summary <- bind_rows(
  summ(var_qual$QUAL)         |> mutate(metric = "Variant Quality (QUAL)"),
  summ(var_depth$MEAN_DEPTH)  |> mutate(metric = "Variant Depth (mean)"),
  summ(var_miss$F_MISS)       |> mutate(metric = "Variant Missingness (fmiss)"),
  summ(var_freq$maf)          |> mutate(metric = "Minor Allele Frequency (MAF)"),
  summ(ind_depth$MEAN_DEPTH)  |> mutate(metric = "Individual Depth"),
  summ(ind_miss$F_MISS)       |> mutate(metric = "Individual Missingness"),
  summ(ind_het$F)             |> mutate(metric = "Inbreeding (F)")
) |> 
  relocate(metric)


#save qc summary as tsv file
write_tsv(qc_full_summary, file = "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/vcf_raw_stats_summary.tsv")
qc_full_summary

qc_full_summary <- read_tsv(
  "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/vcf_raw_stats_summary.tsv"
)

# Rows: 7 Columns: 9
# ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Delimiter: "\t"
# chr (1): metric
# dbl (8): n, min, q1, median, mean, q3, max, sd

# ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
# ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.

kable(qc_full_summary, caption = "Summary statistics for raw VCF")
```

## 12 Filtering

### Filtering by Site 1st

Script: filt_site1.sh

``` bash
#!/bin/bash -l

#SBATCH --job-name=filt_site1
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --time=2:00:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=6
#SBATCH --mem=24gb
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --mail-type=END,FAIL
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/logs/%j_%x.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/logs/%j_%x.err

module purge
module load bio/VCFtools

vcftools    --gzvcf /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/AlandIslandsHM.vcf.gz \
    --recode-INFO-all \
    --mac 3 \
    --max-alleles 2 \
    --max-meanDP 50 \
    --min-alleles 2 \
    --min-meanDP 8 \
    --minQ 30 \
    --max-missing 0.7 \
    --out /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70 \
    --recode \
    --remove-indels

# Now rerunning the vcftools stats

# Make variables
SET_VCF="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70.recode.vcf" 

OUT="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70" # This defines the output

# Calculate the allele frequency
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --freq2 --out $OUT --max-alleles 2 

# Calculate the mean depth per individual
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --depth --out $OUT 

# Calculate the mean depth per site
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --site-mean-depth --out $OUT 

# Calculate the site quality
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --site-quality --out $OUT 

# Calculate the proportion of missing data per individual
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --missing-indv --out $OUT 

# Calculate proportion of missing data per site
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --missing-site --out $OUT 

# Calculate the heterozygosity and inbreeding coefficient per individual
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --het --out $OUT 
```

### Visualisation of VCF Data Filtered by Site 1st

``` r
# VISUALISATION OF THE FILTERED VCF STATS
# R Script
install.packages("tidyverse")
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)

# Define file paths
base <- "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools"

# Helper to read tab files with header + 1 header line to skip
read_tab <- function(path, col_names) {
  vroom::vroom(path, delim = "\t", skip = 1, col_names = col_names, show_col_types = FALSE)
}

# --- Read files ---
var_qual <- read_tab(file.path(base, "minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70.lqual"),
                     c("CHROM","POS","QUAL"))

var_depth <- read_tab(file.path(base, "minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70.ldepth.mean"),
                      c("CHROM","POS","MEAN_DEPTH","VAR_DEPTH"))

var_miss <- read_tab(file.path(base, "minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70.lmiss"),
                     c("CHR","POS","N_DATA","N_GENOTYPE_FILTERED","N_MISS","F_MISS"))

# --freq2 prints allele:freq; parse the numeric frequencies correctly
var_freq_filtered <- read_tab(file.path(base, "minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70.frq"),
                         c("CHR","POS","N_ALLELES","N_CHR","a1","a2"))

parse_freq <- function(x) readr::parse_number(sub(".*:", "", x))

var_freq <- var_freq_filtered %>%
  mutate(freq1 = parse_freq(a1),
         freq2 = parse_freq(a2),
         maf   = pmin(freq1, freq2, na.rm = TRUE))

ind_depth <- read_tab(file.path(base, "minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70.idepth"),
                      c("INDV","N_SITES","MEAN_DEPTH"))

ind_miss  <- read_tab(file.path(base, "minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70.imiss"),
                      c("INDV","N_DATA","N_GENOTYPES_FILTERED","N_MISS","F_MISS"))

ind_het   <- read_tab(file.path(base, "minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70.het"),
                      c("INDV","O(HOM)","E(HOM)","N_SITES","F"))  # F ~ inbreeding coefficient
                      
write.csv(var_qual, file.path(base, "var_qual_output_site1.csv"))
write.csv(var_depth, file.path(base, "var_depth_output_site1.csv"))
write.csv(var_miss, file.path(base, "var_miss_output_site1.csv"))
write.csv(var_freq, file.path(base, "var_freq_output_site1.csv"))
write.csv(ind_depth, file.path(base, "ind_depth_output_site1.csv"))
write.csv(ind_miss, file.path(base, "ind_miss_output_site1.csv"))
write.csv(ind_het, file.path(base, "ind_het_output_site1.csv"))  

# PATCHWORK PLOT OF VARIOUS VCF FILTERED STATS

library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

# Define file paths
base <- "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools"

# Helper to read CSV files
read_csv <- function(path) {
  read.csv(path)
}

# --- Read files (now using the CSV output files) ---
var_qual <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_qual_output_site1.csv"))
var_depth <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_depth_output_site1.csv"))
var_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_miss_output_site1.csv"))
var_freq <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_freq_output_site1.csv"))
ind_depth <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_depth_output_site1.csv"))
ind_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_miss_output_site1.csv"))
ind_het <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_het_output_site1.csv"))

# Quick sanity: confirm the data frames exist & have rows
for (nm in c("var_qual","var_depth","var_miss","var_freq","ind_depth","ind_miss","ind_het")) {
if (!exists(nm)) stop(sprintf("Object '%s' does not exist (check file paths in qc-setup).", nm))
if (!is.data.frame(get(nm)) || nrow(get(nm)) == 0) stop(sprintf("Object '%s' has 0 rows.", nm))
}

# Create plots for each dataset
p1 <- ggplot(var_qual, aes(QUAL)) + 
  geom_density(alpha = .3) +  
  theme_light() + 
  labs(title = "Variant Quality")

p2 <- ggplot(var_depth, aes(MEAN_DEPTH)) + 
  geom_density(alpha = .3) + 
  theme_light() + 
  labs(title = "Variant Depth (mean)")

p3 <- ggplot(var_miss, aes(F_MISS)) + 
  geom_density(alpha = .3) + 
  theme_light() + 
  labs(title = "Variant Missingness (fmiss)")

p4 <- ggplot(var_freq, aes(maf)) + 
  geom_density(alpha = .3) + 
  theme_light() + 
  labs(title = "Minor Allele Frequency (MAF)")

p5 <- ggplot(ind_depth, aes(MEAN_DEPTH)) + 
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, alpha = .3) + 
  theme_light() + 
  labs(title = "Individual Depth")

p6 <- ggplot(ind_miss, aes(F_MISS)) + 
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, alpha = .3) + 
  theme_light() + 
  labs(title = "Individual Missingness")

p7 <- ggplot(ind_het, aes(F)) + 
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, alpha = .3) + 
  theme_light() + 
  labs(title = "Individual Inbreeding (F)")

# Compose robustly (avoids relying on | and / operators)
panel <- patchwork::wrap_plots(list(p1, p2, p3, p4, p5, p6, p7), ncol = 2, guides = "collect")


# Save panel as pdf file
ggsave("/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/vcf_filt_site1_stats.pdf", panel, width = 10, height = 14)

# SUMMARY TABLE OF FILTERED VCF STATS
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(purrr)
library(readr)
library(knitr)

# Helper function to compute full summary stats for a numeric vector
summ <- function(x) {
  tibble(
    n      = sum(!is.na(x)),
    min    = min(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    q1     = quantile(x, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    median = median(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean   = mean(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    q3     = quantile(x, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    max    = max(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd     = sd(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
}

# Define file paths
base <- "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools"

# Helper to read CSV files
read_csv <- function(path) {
  read.csv(path)
}

# --- Read files (now using the CSV output files) ---
var_qual <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_qual_output_site1.csv"))
var_depth <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_depth_output_site1.csv"))
var_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_miss_output_site1.csv"))
var_freq <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_freq_output_site1.csv"))
ind_depth <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_depth_output_site1.csv"))
ind_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_miss_output_site1.csv"))
ind_het <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_het_output_site1.csv"))

# Build summary for each dataset
qc_full_summary <- bind_rows(
  summ(var_qual$QUAL)         |> mutate(metric = "Variant Quality (QUAL)"),
  summ(var_depth$MEAN_DEPTH)  |> mutate(metric = "Variant Depth (mean)"),
  summ(var_miss$F_MISS)       |> mutate(metric = "Variant Missingness (fmiss)"),
  summ(var_freq$maf)          |> mutate(metric = "Minor Allele Frequency (MAF)"),
  summ(ind_depth$MEAN_DEPTH)       |> mutate(metric = "Individual Depth"),
  summ(ind_miss$F_MISS)       |> mutate(metric = "Individual Missingness"),
  summ(ind_het$F)             |> mutate(metric = "Inbreeding (F)")
) |> 
  relocate(metric)


#save qc summary as tsv file
write_tsv(qc_full_summary, file = "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/vcf_filt_site1_stats_summary.tsv")
qc_full_summary

qc_full_summary <- read_tsv(
  "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/vcf_filt_site1_stats_summary.tsv"
)

# Rows: 7 Columns: 9
# ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Delimiter: "\t"
# chr (1): metric
# dbl (8): n, min, q1, median, mean, q3, max, sd

# ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
# ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.

kable(qc_full_summary, caption = "Summary statistics for filtered by site VCF")
```

### Filtering by Individual 2nd

``` r
# Use the filtered file from the filtering by site
library(tidyverse)
high_missing_ids20 <- ind_miss %>%
  filter(F_MISS > 0.2) %>%
  arrange(desc(F_MISS))
# ind_miss was before read into R as a data frame 
# Save high_missing_ids60 as textfile
write_tsv(high_missing_ids20, file = "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70_high_missing_ids20.txt")
```

``` bash
tail -n +2 minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70_high_missing_ids20.txt | cut -f 2 > high_missing_id_20.txt

vcftools \
    --vcf minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70.recode.vcf \
    --remove high_missing_id_20.txt  \
    --recode-INFO-all \
    --out minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70_INDMISS20   \
    --recode
```

Script: filt_indv2_HM.sh

``` bash
#!/bin/bash -l

#SBATCH --job-name=filt_indv2
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --time=2:00:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=6
#SBATCH --mem=24gb
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --mail-type=END,FAIL
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/logs/%j_%x.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/logs/%j_%x.err

module purge
module load bio/VCFtools

# Now rerunning the vcftools stats

# Make variables
SET_VCF="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70_INDMISS20.recode.vcf" 

OUT="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70_INDMISS20"

# Calculate the allele frequency
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --freq2 --out $OUT --max-alleles 2 

# Calculate the mean depth per individual
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --depth --out $OUT 

# Calculate the mean depth per site
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --site-mean-depth --out $OUT 

# Calculate the site quality
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --site-quality --out $OUT 

# Calculate the proportion of missing data per individual
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --missing-indv --out $OUT 

# Calculate proportion of missing data per site
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --missing-site --out $OUT 

# Calculate the heterozygosity and inbreeding coefficient per individual
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --het --out $OUT 
```

### Visualisation of VCF Stats Filtered by Individual 2nd

``` r
# VISUALISATION OF THE FILTERED VCF STATS
# R Script
install.packages("tidyverse")
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)

# Define file paths
base <- "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools"

# Helper to read tab files with header + 1 header line to skip
read_tab <- function(path, col_names) {
  vroom::vroom(path, delim = "\t", skip = 1, col_names = col_names, show_col_types = FALSE)
}

# --- Read files ---
var_qual <- read_tab(file.path(base, "minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70_INDMISS20.lqual"),
                     c("CHROM","POS","QUAL"))

var_depth <- read_tab(file.path(base, "minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70_INDMISS20.ldepth.mean"),
                      c("CHROM","POS","MEAN_DEPTH","VAR_DEPTH"))

var_miss <- read_tab(file.path(base, "minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70_INDMISS20.lmiss"),
                     c("CHR","POS","N_DATA","N_GENOTYPE_FILTERED","N_MISS","F_MISS"))

# --freq2 prints allele:freq; parse the numeric frequencies correctly
var_freq_filtered <- read_tab(file.path(base, "minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70_INDMISS20.frq"),
                         c("CHR","POS","N_ALLELES","N_CHR","a1","a2"))

parse_freq <- function(x) readr::parse_number(sub(".*:", "", x))

var_freq <- var_freq_filtered %>%
  mutate(freq1 = parse_freq(a1),
         freq2 = parse_freq(a2),
         maf   = pmin(freq1, freq2, na.rm = TRUE))

ind_depth <- read_tab(file.path(base, "minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70_INDMISS20.idepth"),
                      c("INDV","N_SITES","MEAN_DEPTH"))

ind_miss  <- read_tab(file.path(base, "minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70_INDMISS20.imiss"),
                      c("INDV","N_DATA","N_GENOTYPES_FILTERED","N_MISS","F_MISS"))

ind_het   <- read_tab(file.path(base, "minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70_INDMISS20.het"),
                      c("INDV","O(HOM)","E(HOM)","N_SITES","F"))  # F ~ inbreeding coefficient
                      
write.csv(var_qual, file.path(base, "var_qual_output_indv2.csv"))
write.csv(var_depth, file.path(base, "var_depth_output_indv2.csv"))
write.csv(var_miss, file.path(base, "var_miss_output_indv2.csv"))
write.csv(var_freq, file.path(base, "var_freq_output_indv2.csv"))
write.csv(ind_depth, file.path(base, "ind_depth_output_indv2.csv"))
write.csv(ind_miss, file.path(base, "ind_miss_output_indv2.csv"))
write.csv(ind_het, file.path(base, "ind_het_output_indv2.csv"))  

# PATCHWORK PLOT OF VARIOUS VCF FILTERED STATS

library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

# Define file paths
base <- "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools"

# Helper to read CSV files
read_csv <- function(path) {
  read.csv(path)
}

# --- Read files (now using the CSV output files) ---
var_qual <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_qual_output_indv2.csv"))
var_depth <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_depth_output_indv2.csv"))
var_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_miss_output_indv2.csv"))
var_freq <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_freq_output_indv2.csv"))
ind_depth <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_depth_output_indv2.csv"))
ind_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_miss_output_indv2.csv"))
ind_het <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_het_output_indv2.csv"))

# Quick sanity: confirm the data frames exist & have rows
for (nm in c("var_qual","var_depth","var_miss","var_freq","ind_depth","ind_miss","ind_het")) {
if (!exists(nm)) stop(sprintf("Object '%s' does not exist (check file paths in qc-setup).", nm))
if (!is.data.frame(get(nm)) || nrow(get(nm)) == 0) stop(sprintf("Object '%s' has 0 rows.", nm))
}

# Create plots for each dataset
p1 <- ggplot(var_qual, aes(QUAL)) + 
  geom_density(alpha = .3) +  
  theme_light() + 
  labs(title = "Variant Quality")

p2 <- ggplot(var_depth, aes(MEAN_DEPTH)) + 
  geom_density(alpha = .3) + 
  theme_light() + 
  labs(title = "Variant Depth (mean)")

p3 <- ggplot(var_miss, aes(F_MISS)) + 
  geom_density(alpha = .3) + 
  theme_light() + 
  labs(title = "Variant Missingness (fmiss)")

p4 <- ggplot(var_freq, aes(maf)) + 
  geom_density(alpha = .3) + 
  theme_light() + 
  labs(title = "Minor Allele Frequency (MAF)")

p5 <- ggplot(ind_depth, aes(MEAN_DEPTH)) + 
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, alpha = .3) + 
  theme_light() + 
  labs(title = "Individual Depth")

p6 <- ggplot(ind_miss, aes(F_MISS)) + 
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, alpha = .3) + 
  theme_light() + 
  labs(title = "Individual Missingness")

p7 <- ggplot(ind_het, aes(F)) + 
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, alpha = .3) + 
  theme_light() + 
  labs(title = "Individual Inbreeding (F)")

# Compose robustly (avoids relying on | and / operators)
panel <- patchwork::wrap_plots(list(p1, p2, p3, p4, p5, p6, p7), ncol = 2, guides = "collect")


# Save panel as pdf file
ggsave("/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/vcf_filt_indv2_stats.pdf", panel, width = 10, height = 14)

# SUMMARY TABLE OF FILTERED VCF STATS
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(purrr)
library(readr)
library(knitr)

# Helper function to compute full summary stats for a numeric vector
summ <- function(x) {
  tibble(
    n      = sum(!is.na(x)),
    min    = min(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    q1     = quantile(x, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    median = median(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean   = mean(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    q3     = quantile(x, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    max    = max(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd     = sd(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
}

# Define file paths
base <- "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools"

# Helper to read CSV files
read_csv <- function(path) {
  read.csv(path)
}

# --- Read files (now using the CSV output files) ---
var_qual <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_qual_output_indv2.csv"))
var_depth <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_depth_output_indv2.csv"))
var_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_miss_output_indv2.csv"))
var_freq <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_freq_output_indv2.csv"))
ind_depth <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_depth_output_indv2.csv"))
ind_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_miss_output_indv2.csv"))
ind_het <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_het_output_indv2.csv"))

# Build summary for each dataset
qc_full_summary <- bind_rows(
  summ(var_qual$QUAL)         |> mutate(metric = "Variant Quality (QUAL)"),
  summ(var_depth$MEAN_DEPTH)  |> mutate(metric = "Variant Depth (mean)"),
  summ(var_miss$F_MISS)       |> mutate(metric = "Variant Missingness (fmiss)"),
  summ(var_freq$maf)          |> mutate(metric = "Minor Allele Frequency (MAF)"),
  summ(ind_depth$MEAN_DEPTH)       |> mutate(metric = "Individual Depth"),
  summ(ind_miss$F_MISS)       |> mutate(metric = "Individual Missingness"),
  summ(ind_het$F)             |> mutate(metric = "Inbreeding (F)")
) |> 
  relocate(metric)


#save qc summary as tsv file
write_tsv(qc_full_summary, file = "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/vcf_filt_indv2_stats_summary.tsv")
qc_full_summary

qc_full_summary <- read_tsv(
  "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/vcf_filt_indv2_stats_summary.tsv"
)

# Rows: 7 Columns: 9
# ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Delimiter: "\t"
# chr (1): metric
# dbl (8): n, min, q1, median, mean, q3, max, sd

# ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
# ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.

kable(qc_full_summary, caption = "Summary statistics for filtered by individual VCF")
```

### Filtering by Individual 1st

Changing the Order: First Filtering by Individual and then Filtering by Site

``` r
# Use the filtered file from the filtering by site
library(tidyverse)
ids20 <- ind_miss %>%
  filter(F_MISS > 0.2) %>%
  arrange(desc(F_MISS))
# ind_miss was before read into R as a data frame 
# Save id_20 as textfile
write_tsv(ids20, file = "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/id_20.txt")
```

``` bash
tail -n +2 id_20.txt | cut -f 2 > ids_20.txt

vcftools \
    --gzvcf /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/AlandIslandsHM.vcf.gz \
    --remove id_20.txt  \
    --recode-INFO-all \
    --out INDMISS20   \
    --recode
```

``` bash
# Make variables
SET_VCF="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70_INDMISS20.recode.vcf" 

OUT="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70_INDMISS20"

# Calculate the allele frequency
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --freq2 --out $OUT --max-alleles 2 

# Calculate the mean depth per individual
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --depth --out $OUT 

# Calculate the mean depth per site
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --site-mean-depth --out $OUT 

# Calculate the site quality
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --site-quality --out $OUT 

# Calculate the proportion of missing data per individual
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --missing-indv --out $OUT 

# Calculate proportion of missing data per site
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --missing-site --out $OUT 

# Calculate the heterozygosity and inbreeding coefficient per individual
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --het --out $OUT 
```

### Visualisation of Filtered by Individual 1st VCF Stats

``` r
# VISUALISATION OF THE FILTERED VCF STATS
# R Script
install.packages("tidyverse")
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)

# Define file paths
base <- "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools"

# Helper to read tab files with header + 1 header line to skip
read_tab <- function(path, col_names) {
  vroom::vroom(path, delim = "\t", skip = 1, col_names = col_names, show_col_types = FALSE)
}

# --- Read files ---
var_qual <- read_tab(file.path(base, "minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70_INDMISS20.lqual"),
                     c("CHROM","POS","QUAL"))

var_depth <- read_tab(file.path(base, "minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70_INDMISS20.ldepth.mean"),
                      c("CHROM","POS","MEAN_DEPTH","VAR_DEPTH"))

var_miss <- read_tab(file.path(base, "minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70_INDMISS20.lmiss"),
                     c("CHR","POS","N_DATA","N_GENOTYPE_FILTERED","N_MISS","F_MISS"))

# --freq2 prints allele:freq; parse the numeric frequencies correctly
var_freq_filtered <- read_tab(file.path(base, "minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70_INDMISS20.frq"),
                         c("CHR","POS","N_ALLELES","N_CHR","a1","a2"))

parse_freq <- function(x) readr::parse_number(sub(".*:", "", x))

var_freq <- var_freq_filtered %>%
  mutate(freq1 = parse_freq(a1),
         freq2 = parse_freq(a2),
         maf   = pmin(freq1, freq2, na.rm = TRUE))

ind_depth <- read_tab(file.path(base, "minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70_INDMISS20.idepth"),
                      c("INDV","N_SITES","MEAN_DEPTH"))

ind_miss  <- read_tab(file.path(base, "minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70_INDMISS20.imiss"),
                      c("INDV","N_DATA","N_GENOTYPES_FILTERED","N_MISS","F_MISS"))

ind_het   <- read_tab(file.path(base, "minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70_INDMISS20.het"),
                      c("INDV","O(HOM)","E(HOM)","N_SITES","F"))  # F ~ inbreeding coefficient
                      
write.csv(var_qual, file.path(base, "var_qual_output_indv1.csv"))
write.csv(var_depth, file.path(base, "var_depth_output_indv1.csv"))
write.csv(var_miss, file.path(base, "var_miss_output_indv1.csv"))
write.csv(var_freq, file.path(base, "var_freq_output_indv1.csv"))
write.csv(ind_depth, file.path(base, "ind_depth_output_indv1.csv"))
write.csv(ind_miss, file.path(base, "ind_miss_output_indv1.csv"))
write.csv(ind_het, file.path(base, "ind_het_output_indv1.csv"))  

# PATCHWORK PLOT OF VARIOUS VCF FILTERED STATS

library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

# Define file paths
base <- "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools"

# Helper to read CSV files
read_csv <- function(path) {
  read.csv(path)
}

# --- Read files (now using the CSV output files) ---
var_qual <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_qual_output_indv1.csv"))
var_depth <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_depth_output_indv1.csv"))
var_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_miss_output_indv1.csv"))
var_freq <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_freq_output_indv1.csv"))
ind_depth <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_depth_output_indv1.csv"))
ind_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_miss_output_indv1.csv"))
ind_het <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_het_output_indv1.csv"))

# Quick sanity: confirm the data frames exist & have rows
for (nm in c("var_qual","var_depth","var_miss","var_freq","ind_depth","ind_miss","ind_het")) {
if (!exists(nm)) stop(sprintf("Object '%s' does not exist (check file paths in qc-setup).", nm))
if (!is.data.frame(get(nm)) || nrow(get(nm)) == 0) stop(sprintf("Object '%s' has 0 rows.", nm))
}

# Create plots for each dataset
p1 <- ggplot(var_qual, aes(QUAL)) + 
  geom_density(alpha = .3) +  
  theme_light() + 
  labs(title = "Variant Quality")

p2 <- ggplot(var_depth, aes(MEAN_DEPTH)) + 
  geom_density(alpha = .3) + 
  theme_light() + 
  labs(title = "Variant Depth (mean)")

p3 <- ggplot(var_miss, aes(F_MISS)) + 
  geom_density(alpha = .3) + 
  theme_light() + 
  labs(title = "Variant Missingness (fmiss)")

p4 <- ggplot(var_freq, aes(maf)) + 
  geom_density(alpha = .3) + 
  theme_light() + 
  labs(title = "Minor Allele Frequency (MAF)")

p5 <- ggplot(ind_depth, aes(MEAN_DEPTH)) + 
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, alpha = .3) + 
  theme_light() + 
  labs(title = "Individual Depth")

p6 <- ggplot(ind_miss, aes(F_MISS)) + 
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, alpha = .3) + 
  theme_light() + 
  labs(title = "Individual Missingness")

p7 <- ggplot(ind_het, aes(F)) + 
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, alpha = .3) + 
  theme_light() + 
  labs(title = "Individual Inbreeding (F)")

# Compose robustly (avoids relying on | and / operators)
panel <- patchwork::wrap_plots(list(p1, p2, p3, p4, p5, p6, p7), ncol = 2, guides = "collect")


# Save panel as pdf file
ggsave("/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/vcf_filt_indv1_stats.pdf", panel, width = 10, height = 14)

# SUMMARY TABLE OF FILTERED VCF STATS
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(purrr)
library(readr)
library(knitr)

# Helper function to compute full summary stats for a numeric vector
summ <- function(x) {
  tibble(
    n      = sum(!is.na(x)),
    min    = min(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    q1     = quantile(x, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    median = median(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean   = mean(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    q3     = quantile(x, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    max    = max(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd     = sd(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
}

# Define file paths
base <- "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools"

# Helper to read CSV files
read_csv <- function(path) {
  read.csv(path)
}

# --- Read files (now using the CSV output files) ---
var_qual <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_qual_output_indv1.csv"))
var_depth <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_depth_output_indv1.csv"))
var_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_miss_output_indv1.csv"))
var_freq <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_freq_output_indv1.csv"))
ind_depth <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_depth_output_indv1.csv"))
ind_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_miss_output_indv1.csv"))
ind_het <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_het_output_indv1.csv"))

# Build summary for each dataset
qc_full_summary <- bind_rows(
  summ(var_qual$QUAL)         |> mutate(metric = "Variant Quality (QUAL)"),
  summ(var_depth$MEAN_DEPTH)  |> mutate(metric = "Variant Depth (mean)"),
  summ(var_miss$F_MISS)       |> mutate(metric = "Variant Missingness (fmiss)"),
  summ(var_freq$maf)          |> mutate(metric = "Minor Allele Frequency (MAF)"),
  summ(ind_depth$MEAN_DEPTH)       |> mutate(metric = "Individual Depth"),
  summ(ind_miss$F_MISS)       |> mutate(metric = "Individual Missingness"),
  summ(ind_het$F)             |> mutate(metric = "Inbreeding (F)")
) |> 
  relocate(metric)


#save qc summary as tsv file
write_tsv(qc_full_summary, file = "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/vcf_filt_indv1_stats_summary.tsv")
qc_full_summary

qc_full_summary <- read_tsv(
  "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/vcf_filt_indv1_stats_summary.tsv"
)

# Rows: 7 Columns: 9
# ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Delimiter: "\t"
# chr (1): metric
# dbl (8): n, min, q1, median, mean, q3, max, sd

# ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
# ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.

kable(qc_full_summary, caption = "Summary statistics for filtered by site VCF")
```

### Filtering by Site 2nd

``` bash
#!/bin/bash -l

#SBATCH --job-name=filt_site2
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --time=2:00:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=6
#SBATCH --mem=24gb
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --mail-type=END,FAIL
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/logs/%j_%x.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/logs/%j_%x.err

module purge
module load bio/VCFtools

vcftools    --vcf /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/INDMISS20.recode.vcf \
    --recode-INFO-all \
    --mac 3 \
    --min-alleles 2 \
    --max-alleles 2 \
    --min-meanDP 5 \
    --max-meanDP 40 \
    --minDP 5 \
    --minQ 30 \
    --max-missing 0.9 \
    --out /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss90_INDMISS20 \
    --recode \
    --remove-indels

# Make variables
SET_VCF="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss90_INDMISS20.recode.vcf" 
OUT="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss90_INDMISS20" 

# Calculate the allele frequency
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --freq2 --out $OUT --max-alleles 2 

# Calculate the mean depth per individual
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --depth --out $OUT 

# Calculate the mean depth per site
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --site-mean-depth --out $OUT 

# Calculate the site quality
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --site-quality --out $OUT 

# Calculate the proportion of missing data per individual
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --missing-indv --out $OUT 

# Calculate proportion of missing data per site
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --missing-site --out $OUT 

# Calculate the heterozygosity and inbreeding coefficient per individual
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --het --out $OUT 
```

### Visualisation of VCF Filtered by Individual 1st and Site 2nd

``` r
# VISUALISATION OF THE FILTERED VCF STATS
# R Script
install.packages("tidyverse")
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)

# Define file paths
base <- "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools"

# Helper to read tab files with header + 1 header line to skip
read_tab <- function(path, col_names) {
  vroom::vroom(path, delim = "\t", skip = 1, col_names = col_names, show_col_types = FALSE)
}

# --- Read files ---
var_qual <- read_tab(file.path(base, "minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss90_INDMISS20.lqual"),
                     c("CHROM","POS","QUAL"))

var_depth <- read_tab(file.path(base, "minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss90_INDMISS20.ldepth.mean"),
                      c("CHROM","POS","MEAN_DEPTH","VAR_DEPTH"))

var_miss <- read_tab(file.path(base, "minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss90_INDMISS20.lmiss"),
                     c("CHR","POS","N_DATA","N_GENOTYPE_FILTERED","N_MISS","F_MISS"))

# --freq2 prints allele:freq; parse the numeric frequencies correctly
var_freq_filtered <- read_tab(file.path(base, "minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss90_INDMISS20.frq"),
                         c("CHR","POS","N_ALLELES","N_CHR","a1","a2"))

parse_freq <- function(x) readr::parse_number(sub(".*:", "", x))

var_freq <- var_freq_filtered %>%
  mutate(freq1 = parse_freq(a1),
         freq2 = parse_freq(a2),
         maf   = pmin(freq1, freq2, na.rm = TRUE))

ind_depth <- read_tab(file.path(base, "minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss90_INDMISS20.idepth"),
                      c("INDV","N_SITES","MEAN_DEPTH"))

ind_miss  <- read_tab(file.path(base, "minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss90_INDMISS20.imiss"),
                      c("INDV","N_DATA","N_GENOTYPES_FILTERED","N_MISS","F_MISS"))

ind_het   <- read_tab(file.path(base, "minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss90_INDMISS20.het"),
                      c("INDV","O(HOM)","E(HOM)","N_SITES","F"))  # F ~ inbreeding coefficient
                      
write.csv(var_qual, file.path(base, "var_qual_output_site2.csv"))
write.csv(var_depth, file.path(base, "var_depth_output_site2.csv"))
write.csv(var_miss, file.path(base, "var_miss_output_site2.csv"))
write.csv(var_freq, file.path(base, "var_freq_output_site2.csv"))
write.csv(ind_depth, file.path(base, "ind_depth_output_site2.csv"))
write.csv(ind_miss, file.path(base, "ind_miss_output_site2.csv"))
write.csv(ind_het, file.path(base, "ind_het_output_site2.csv"))  

# PATCHWORK PLOT OF VARIOUS VCF FILTERED STATS

library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

# Define file paths
base <- "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools"

# Helper to read CSV files
read_csv <- function(path) {
  read.csv(path)
}

# --- Read files (now using the CSV output files) ---
var_qual <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_qual_output_site2.csv"))
var_depth <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_depth_output_site2.csv"))
var_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_miss_output_site2.csv"))
var_freq <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_freq_output_site2.csv"))
ind_depth <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_depth_output_site2.csv"))
ind_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_miss_output_site2.csv"))
ind_het <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_het_output_site2.csv"))

# Quick sanity: confirm the data frames exist & have rows
for (nm in c("var_qual","var_depth","var_miss","var_freq","ind_depth","ind_miss","ind_het")) {
if (!exists(nm)) stop(sprintf("Object '%s' does not exist (check file paths in qc-setup).", nm))
if (!is.data.frame(get(nm)) || nrow(get(nm)) == 0) stop(sprintf("Object '%s' has 0 rows.", nm))
}

# Create plots for each dataset
p1 <- ggplot(var_qual, aes(QUAL)) + 
  geom_density(alpha = .3) +  
  theme_light() + 
  labs(title = "Variant Quality")

p2 <- ggplot(var_depth, aes(MEAN_DEPTH)) + 
  geom_density(alpha = .3) + 
  theme_light() + 
  labs(title = "Variant Depth (mean)")

p3 <- ggplot(var_miss, aes(F_MISS)) + 
  geom_density(alpha = .3) + 
  theme_light() + 
  labs(title = "Variant Missingness (fmiss)")

p4 <- ggplot(var_freq, aes(maf)) + 
  geom_density(alpha = .3) + 
  theme_light() + 
  labs(title = "Minor Allele Frequency (MAF)")

p5 <- ggplot(ind_depth, aes(MEAN_DEPTH)) + 
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, alpha = .3) + 
  theme_light() + 
  labs(title = "Individual Depth")

p6 <- ggplot(ind_miss, aes(F_MISS)) + 
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, alpha = .3) + 
  theme_light() + 
  labs(title = "Individual Missingness")

p7 <- ggplot(ind_het, aes(F)) + 
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, alpha = .3) + 
  theme_light() + 
  labs(title = "Individual Inbreeding (F)")

# Compose robustly
panel <- patchwork::wrap_plots(list(p1, p2, p3, p4, p5, p6, p7), ncol = 2, guides = "collect")


# Save panel as pdf file
ggsave("/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/vcf_filt_site2_stats.pdf", panel, width = 10, height = 14)

# SUMMARY TABLE OF FILTERED VCF STATS
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(purrr)
library(readr)
library(knitr)

# Helper function to compute full summary stats for a numeric vector
summ <- function(x) {
  tibble(
    n      = sum(!is.na(x)),
    min    = min(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    q1     = quantile(x, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    median = median(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean   = mean(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    q3     = quantile(x, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    max    = max(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd     = sd(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
}

# Define file paths
base <- "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools"

# Helper to read CSV files
read_csv <- function(path) {
  read.csv(path)
}

# --- Read files (now using the CSV output files) ---
var_qual <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_qual_output_site2.csv"))
var_depth <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_depth_output_site2.csv"))
var_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_miss_output_site2.csv"))
var_freq <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_freq_output_site2.csv"))
ind_depth <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_depth_output_site2.csv"))
ind_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_miss_output_site2.csv"))
ind_het <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_het_output_site2.csv"))

# Build summary for each dataset
qc_full_summary <- bind_rows(
  summ(var_qual$QUAL)         |> mutate(metric = "Variant Quality (QUAL)"),
  summ(var_depth$MEAN_DEPTH)  |> mutate(metric = "Variant Depth (mean)"),
  summ(var_miss$F_MISS)       |> mutate(metric = "Variant Missingness (fmiss)"),
  summ(var_freq$maf)          |> mutate(metric = "Minor Allele Frequency (MAF)"),
  summ(ind_depth$MEAN_DEPTH)       |> mutate(metric = "Individual Depth"),
  summ(ind_miss$F_MISS)       |> mutate(metric = "Individual Missingness"),
  summ(ind_het$F)             |> mutate(metric = "Inbreeding (F)")
) |> 
  relocate(metric)


#save qc summary as tsv file
write_tsv(qc_full_summary, file = "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/vcf_filt_site2_stats_summary.tsv")
qc_full_summary

qc_full_summary <- read_tsv(
  "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/vcf_filt_site2_stats_summary.tsv"
)

# Rows: 7 Columns: 9
# ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Delimiter: "\t"
# chr (1): metric
# dbl (8): n, min, q1, median, mean, q3, max, sd

# ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
# ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.

kable(qc_full_summary, caption = "Summary statistics for filtered by site VCF")
```

### Refiltering those individuals with empty data

``` bash
# Finding Individuals with 0 or near 0 sites
awk 'NR>1 && $3==0 {print $1}' \
minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss90_INDMISS20.idepth \
> bad_ind_zero_sites.txt

# Finding High-Missing Individuals
awk 'NR>1 && $5 > 0.2 {print $1}' \
minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss90_INDMISS20.imiss \
> bad_ind_high_missing.txt

# Combining both Lists
cat bad_ind_zero_sites.txt bad_ind_high_missing.txt | sort | uniq > bad_ind.txt

module purge
module load bio/VCFtools

# Removing bad Individuals from VCF
vcftools \
  --vcf /scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss90_INDMISS20.recode.vcf \
  --remove bad_ind.txt \
  --recode \
  --recode-INFO-all \
  --out PCA_ready_indfiltered
  
# Quick QC Check
vcftools --vcf PCA_ready_indfiltered.recode.vcf --missing-indv --out PCA_ready_check
vcftools --vcf PCA_ready_indfiltered.recode.vcf --depth --out PCA_ready_check
vcftools --vcf PCA_ready_indfiltered.recode.vcf --site-mean-depth --out PCA_ready_check
```

``` bash
#!/bin/bash -l

#SBATCH --job-name=filt_indv3
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --time=2:00:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=6
#SBATCH --mem=24gb
#SBATCH --account=ag-demeaux
#SBATCH --mail-type=END,FAIL
#SBATCH --output=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/logs/%j_%x.out
#SBATCH --error=/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/logs/%j_%x.err

module purge
module load bio/VCFtools

# Make variables
SET_VCF="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/PCA_ready_indfiltered.recode.vcf" 
OUT="/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/PCA_ready_indfiltered" 

# Calculate the allele frequency
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --freq2 --out $OUT --max-alleles 2 

# Calculate the mean depth per individual
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --depth --out $OUT 

# Calculate the mean depth per site
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --site-mean-depth --out $OUT 

# Calculate the site quality
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --site-quality --out $OUT 

# Calculate the proportion of missing data per individual
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --missing-indv --out $OUT 

# Calculate proportion of missing data per site
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --missing-site --out $OUT 

# Calculate the heterozygosity and inbreeding coefficient per individual
vcftools --vcf $SET_VCF --het --out $OUT 
```

### Redoing Visualisation for Re-filtered VCF by Individual 3rd

``` r
# VISUALISATION OF THE FILTERED VCF STATS
# R Script
install.packages("tidyverse")
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)

# Define file paths
base <- "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools"

# Helper to read tab files with header + 1 header line to skip
read_tab <- function(path, col_names) {
  vroom::vroom(path, delim = "\t", skip = 1, col_names = col_names, show_col_types = FALSE)
}

# --- Read files ---
var_qual <- read_tab(file.path(base, "PCA_ready_indfiltered.lqual"),
                     c("CHROM","POS","QUAL"))

var_depth <- read_tab(file.path(base, "PCA_ready_indfiltered.ldepth.mean"),
                      c("CHROM","POS","MEAN_DEPTH","VAR_DEPTH"))

var_miss <- read_tab(file.path(base, "PCA_ready_indfiltered.lmiss"),
                     c("CHR","POS","N_DATA","N_GENOTYPE_FILTERED","N_MISS","F_MISS"))

# --freq2 prints allele:freq; parse the numeric frequencies correctly
var_freq_filtered <- read_tab(file.path(base, "PCA_ready_indfiltered.frq"),
                         c("CHR","POS","N_ALLELES","N_CHR","a1","a2"))

parse_freq <- function(x) readr::parse_number(sub(".*:", "", x))

var_freq <- var_freq_filtered %>%
  mutate(freq1 = parse_freq(a1),
         freq2 = parse_freq(a2),
         maf   = pmin(freq1, freq2, na.rm = TRUE))

ind_depth <- read_tab(file.path(base, "PCA_ready_indfiltered.idepth"),
                      c("INDV","N_SITES","MEAN_DEPTH"))

ind_miss  <- read_tab(file.path(base, "PCA_ready_indfiltered.imiss"),
                      c("INDV","N_DATA","N_GENOTYPES_FILTERED","N_MISS","F_MISS"))

ind_het   <- read_tab(file.path(base, "PCA_ready_indfiltered.het"),
                      c("INDV","O(HOM)","E(HOM)","N_SITES","F"))  # F ~ inbreeding coefficient
                      
write.csv(var_qual, file.path(base, "var_qual_output_indv3.csv"))
write.csv(var_depth, file.path(base, "var_depth_output_indv3.csv"))
write.csv(var_miss, file.path(base, "var_miss_output_indv3.csv"))
write.csv(var_freq, file.path(base, "var_freq_output_indv3.csv"))
write.csv(ind_depth, file.path(base, "ind_depth_output_indv3.csv"))
write.csv(ind_miss, file.path(base, "ind_miss_output_indv3.csv"))
write.csv(ind_het, file.path(base, "ind_het_output_indv3.csv"))  

# PATCHWORK PLOT OF VARIOUS VCF FILTERED STATS

library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

# Define file paths
base <- "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools"

# Helper to read CSV files
read_csv <- function(path) {
  read.csv(path)
}

# --- Read files (now using the CSV output files) ---
var_qual <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_qual_output_indv3.csv"))
var_depth <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_depth_output_indv3.csv"))
var_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_miss_output_indv3.csv"))
var_freq <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_freq_output_indv3.csv"))
ind_depth <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_depth_output_indv3.csv"))
ind_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_miss_output_indv3.csv"))
ind_het <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_het_output_indv3.csv"))

# Quick sanity: confirm the data frames exist & have rows
for (nm in c("var_qual","var_depth","var_miss","var_freq","ind_depth","ind_miss","ind_het")) {
if (!exists(nm)) stop(sprintf("Object '%s' does not exist (check file paths in qc-setup).", nm))
if (!is.data.frame(get(nm)) || nrow(get(nm)) == 0) stop(sprintf("Object '%s' has 0 rows.", nm))
}

# Create plots for each dataset
p1 <- ggplot(var_qual, aes(QUAL)) + 
  geom_density(alpha = .3) +  
  theme_light() + 
  labs(title = "Variant Quality")

p2 <- ggplot(var_depth, aes(MEAN_DEPTH)) + 
  geom_density(alpha = .3) + 
  theme_light() + 
  labs(title = "Variant Depth (mean)")

p3 <- ggplot(var_miss, aes(F_MISS)) + 
  geom_density(alpha = .3) + 
  theme_light() + 
  labs(title = "Variant Missingness (fmiss)")

p4 <- ggplot(var_freq, aes(maf)) + 
  geom_density(alpha = .3) + 
  theme_light() + 
  labs(title = "Minor Allele Frequency (MAF)")

p5 <- ggplot(ind_depth, aes(MEAN_DEPTH)) + 
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, alpha = .3) + 
  theme_light() + 
  labs(title = "Individual Depth")

p6 <- ggplot(ind_miss, aes(F_MISS)) + 
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, alpha = .3) + 
  theme_light() + 
  labs(title = "Individual Missingness")

p7 <- ggplot(ind_het, aes(F)) + 
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, alpha = .3) + 
  theme_light() + 
  labs(title = "Individual Inbreeding (F)")

# Compose robustly
panel <- patchwork::wrap_plots(list(p1, p2, p3, p4, p5, p6, p7), ncol = 2, guides = "collect")


# Save panel as pdf file
ggsave("/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/vcf_filt_indv3_stats.pdf", panel, width = 10, height = 14)

# SUMMARY TABLE OF FILTERED VCF STATS
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(purrr)
library(readr)
library(knitr)

# Helper function to compute full summary stats for a numeric vector
summ <- function(x) {
  tibble(
    n      = sum(!is.na(x)),
    min    = min(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    q1     = quantile(x, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    median = median(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean   = mean(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    q3     = quantile(x, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    max    = max(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd     = sd(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
}

# Define file paths
base <- "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools"

# Helper to read CSV files
read_csv <- function(path) {
  read.csv(path)
}

# --- Read files (now using the CSV output files) ---
var_qual <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_qual_output_indv3.csv"))
var_depth <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_depth_output_indv3.csv"))
var_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_miss_output_indv3.csv"))
var_freq <- read_csv(file.path(base, "var_freq_output_indv3.csv"))
ind_depth <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_depth_output_indv3.csv"))
ind_miss <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_miss_output_indv3.csv"))
ind_het <- read_csv(file.path(base, "ind_het_output_indv3.csv"))

# Build summary for each dataset
qc_full_summary <- bind_rows(
  summ(var_qual$QUAL)         |> mutate(metric = "Variant Quality (QUAL)"),
  summ(var_depth$MEAN_DEPTH)  |> mutate(metric = "Variant Depth (mean)"),
  summ(var_miss$F_MISS)       |> mutate(metric = "Variant Missingness (fmiss)"),
  summ(var_freq$maf)          |> mutate(metric = "Minor Allele Frequency (MAF)"),
  summ(ind_depth$MEAN_DEPTH)       |> mutate(metric = "Individual Depth"),
  summ(ind_miss$F_MISS)       |> mutate(metric = "Individual Missingness"),
  summ(ind_het$F)             |> mutate(metric = "Inbreeding (F)")
) |> 
  relocate(metric)


#save qc summary as tsv file
write_tsv(qc_full_summary, file = "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/vcf_filt_indv3_stats_summary.tsv")
qc_full_summary

qc_full_summary <- read_tsv(
  "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/vcf_filt_indv3_stats_summary.tsv"
)

# Rows: 7 Columns: 9
# ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Delimiter: "\t"
# chr (1): metric
# dbl (8): n, min, q1, median, mean, q3, max, sd

# ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
# ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.

kable(qc_full_summary, caption = "Summary statistics for refiltered by individual VCF")
```

## 13 PCA

Filtering by Site 1st and Individual 2nd

``` bash
bcftools query -l minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70_INDMISS20.recode.vcf

bcftools query -l minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70_INDMISS20.recode.vcf | awk -F'/' '{print $NF}' | awk -F'.sorted.bam' '{print $1}' | awk -F'_' '{print $2 "_" $1 "_" $3}' > new_sample_names_s1i2.txt

bcftools query -l minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70_INDMISS20.recode.vcf | awk -F'/' '{print $NF}' | awk -F'.sorted.bam' '{print $1}' | awk -F'_' '{print $2 "_" $1 "_" $4}' > new_sample_names_s1i2.txt

bcftools query -l minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70_INDMISS20.recode.vcf > old_sample_names_s1i2.txt

paste old_sample_names_s1i2.txt new_sample_names_s1i2.txt > rename_samples_s1i2.txt

bcftools reheader -s rename_samples_s1i2.txt minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70_INDMISS20.recode.vcf -o minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70_INDMISS20_shortnames.vcf

bcftools query -l minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70_INDMISS20_shortnames.vcf

plink --vcf minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70_INDMISS20_shortnames.vcf --double-id --make-bed --allow-extra-chr --out minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70_INDMISS20

plink --bfile minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70_INDMISS20 --pca --allow-extra-chr --out minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70_INDMISS20_pca
```

``` r
install.packages("readr")
install.packages("tidyverse")
install.packages("ggrepel")
library(tidyverse)
library(readr)
library(ggrepel)

# --- Read eigenvectors ---
pca <- read_table(
    "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70_INDMISS20_pca.eigenvec", 
    col_names = FALSE
    )

colnames(pca) <- c("FID", "IID", paste0("PC", 1:20))

# --- Create group column from the FID column ---
pca$group <- sub("_.*", "", pca$FID)  # Everything before first underscore

# Check the new column
pca[, c("FID", "group")]


library(RColorBrewer)

group_levels <- unique(pca$group)

group_colors <- setNames(
  colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(12, "Set3"))(length(group_levels)),
  group_levels
)

# This one is identical to the pca1_2_primulaveris.pdf one
pca_plot <- ggplot(pca, aes(PC1, PC2, color = group)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_color_manual(values = group_colors) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(title = "PCA PC1-PC2")

# --- Save to file directly ---
ggsave(
  filename = "PCA_PC1_PC2.pdf",  # file name
  plot = pca_plot,
  width = 6,    # width in inches
  height = 5,   # height in inches
  dpi = 300     # resolution
)

# --- Read eigenvalues (correct file!) ---
eigval <- scan(
  "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/minQ30_biallelic_noINDELS_mac3_maxmiss70_INDMISS20_pca.eigenval"
)


# --- Compute percentage variance explained ---
percent <- eigval / sum(eigval) * 100

# --- PCA plot ---
pca1_2 <- ggplot(pca, aes(PC1, PC2, color = group)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = FID), size = 3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = group_colors) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    title = "PCA Hypericum pulchrum 1-2",
    x = paste0("PC1 (", round(percent[1], 2), "%)"),
    y = paste0("PC2 (", round(percent[2], 2), "%)")
  )

pca1_3 <- ggplot(pca, aes(PC1, PC3, color = group)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = FID), size = 3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = group_colors) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    title = "PCA Hypericum pulchrum 1-3",
    x = paste0("PC1 (", round(percent[1], 2), "%)"),
    y = paste0("PC3 (", round(percent[3], 2), "%)")
  )
  
pca2_3 <- ggplot(pca, aes(PC2, PC3, color = group)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = FID), size = 3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = group_colors) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    title = "PCA Hypericum pulchrum 2-3",
    x = paste0("PC2 (", round(percent[2], 2), "%)"),
    y = paste0("PC3 (", round(percent[3], 2), "%)")
  )

#save plots as pdf files
ggsave("/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/pdf/pca1_2_hypericumpulchrum.pdf", pca1_2, width = 8, height = 6)
ggsave("/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/pdf/pca1_3_hypericumpulchrum.pdf", pca1_3, width = 8, height = 6)
ggsave("/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/pdf/pca2_3_hypericumpulchrum.pdf", pca2_3, width = 8, height = 6)
```

Filtering by Individual 1st, Site 2nd and Re-filtering by Individual 3rd

``` bash
# 1. extract sample names
bcftools query -l PCA_ready_indfiltered.recode.vcf > old.txt

# 2. create CLEAN names (SAFE)
awk -F'/' '{print $NF}' old.txt | \
sed -E 's/_sorted.*$//' > new.txt

# 3. safety check (MUST be empty)
sort new.txt | uniq -d

# 4. build mapping
paste old.txt new.txt > rename.txt

# 5. reheader VCF
bcftools reheader -s rename.txt \
  PCA_ready_indfiltered.recode.vcf \
  -o PCA_ready_indfiltered_clean.vcf

# 6. verify
bcftools query -l PCA_ready_indfiltered_clean.vcf | head

# 7. check duplicates
bcftools query -l PCA_ready_indfiltered_clean.vcf | sort | uniq -c | sort -nr | head

plink --vcf PCA_ready_indfiltered_clean.vcf \
      --double-id \
      --make-bed \
      --allow-extra-chr \
      --out PCA_ready_indfiltered_clean

plink --bfile PCA_ready_indfiltered_clean \
      --pca \
      --allow-extra-chr \
      --out PCA_ready_indfiltered_clean
```

Making the PCA

``` r
install.packages("readr")
install.packages("tidyverse")
install.packages("ggrepel")
library(tidyverse)
library(readr)
library(ggrepel)

# --- Read eigenvectors ---
pca <- read_table(
    "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/PCA_ready_indfiltered_clean.eigenvec", 
    col_names = FALSE
    )

colnames(pca) <- c("FID", "IID", paste0("PC", 1:20))

# --- Create group column from the FID column ---
pca$group <- sub("_.*", "", pca$FID)

# Check the new column
pca[, c("FID", "group")]


library(RColorBrewer)

group_levels <- sort(unique(pca$group))
pca$group <- factor(pca$group, levels = group_levels)

group_colors <- setNames(
  colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(12, "Set3"))(length(group_levels)),
  group_levels
)

pca_plot <- ggplot(pca, aes(PC1, PC2, color = group)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_color_manual(values = group_colors) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(title = "PCA PC1-PC2")

# --- Save to file directly ---
ggsave(
  filename = "PCA_PC1_PC2.pdf",  # file name
  plot = pca_plot,
  width = 6,    # width in inches
  height = 5,   # height in inches
  dpi = 300     # resolution
)

# --- Read eigenvalues (correct file!) ---
eigval <- scan(
  "/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/vcftools/PCA_ready_indfiltered_clean.eigenval"
)


# --- Compute percentage variance explained ---
percent <- eigval / sum(eigval) * 100

# --- PCA plot ---
pca1_2 <- ggplot(pca, aes(PC1, PC2, color = group)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = FID), size = 3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = group_colors) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    title = "PCA Hypericum pulchrum 1-2",
    x = paste0("PC1 (", round(percent[1], 2), "%)"),
    y = paste0("PC2 (", round(percent[2], 2), "%)")
  )

pca1_3 <- ggplot(pca, aes(PC1, PC3, color = group)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = FID), size = 3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = group_colors) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    title = "PCA Hypericum pulchrum 1-3",
    x = paste0("PC1 (", round(percent[1], 2), "%)"),
    y = paste0("PC3 (", round(percent[3], 2), "%)")
  )
  
pca2_3 <- ggplot(pca, aes(PC2, PC3, color = group)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = FID), size = 3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = group_colors) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    title = "PCA Hypericum pulchrum 2-3",
    x = paste0("PC2 (", round(percent[2], 2), "%)"),
    y = paste0("PC3 (", round(percent[3], 2), "%)")
  )

#save plots as pdf files
ggsave("/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/pdf/pca1_2_hypericumpulchrum_indv3.pdf", pca1_2, width = 8, height = 6)
ggsave("/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/pdf/pca1_3_hypericumpulchrum_indv3.pdf", pca1_3, width = 8, height = 6)
ggsave("/scratch/PNeuhaus/PaulaNeuhaus/Output/mapping/vcf/HM/pdf/pca2_3_hypericumpulchrum_indv3.pdf", pca2_3, width = 8, height = 6)
```

## 14 Counting SNPs

``` bash
bcftools view -H name_of_last_vcf | wc -l
```
